book.title_en: A Glass Bridge Between Two Worlds
book.title_ja: ビーズの魔法：ムラノと京都をつなぐガラスの橋
book.author: Maurizio Lotter

editorial_purpose.function: the book is a dual-field artifact that sustains Murano and Kyoto as coexisting perceptual systems
editorial_purpose.constraint: no translation hierarchy is allowed: EN and JA are co-equal activation layers of the same object

reading_principle.rule_01: EN and JA are parallel fields, not translations
reading_principle.rule_02: photography is a third autonomous perceptual system, not illustration
reading_principle.rule_03: technical sections require slowed perception to activate embodied knowledge traces
reading_principle.rule_04: preface functions as entry into the field, not explanation of it
reading_principle.rule_05: glossary is structural proof of irreducible dual naming

linear_structure.note: opening: the Murano furnace as the originary field — why the glass bridge begins here
migration: the Kyoto workshop as the second pole — the bridge made spatial
transmission: the Venice meeting as the scene where the field travels from maker to receiver
revelation: Como as the space where distance enables perception of the bead's full field
embodiment: the necklace worn as the most intimate form of the cultural encounter

basho.rule: page layout is a spatial field, not a translation structure
basho.constraint: EN and JA must maintain equal visual and semantic weight

kokyu.rule: reading alternates between narrative, technique, and image-based suspension
kokyu.effect: reader experiences craft as temporal modulation rather than linear story

yūgen.rule: photographs contain the irreducible remainder of the system
yūgen.effect: what cannot be said is displaced into visual density

non_rendered_layer.definition: structural presences not explicitly represented in text
contains: embodied knowledge of glassmaking transmitted through gesture
interstitial EN–JA field that produces meaning without translation
historical fragility of Venetian beadmaking tradition
Kyoto as long-duration practice field (30+ years) embedded in objects

reader_effect.state_after_reading: reader internalizes a dual spatial orientation (Murano / Kyoto)
reader_effect.temporal_effect: future encounters with glass reactivate the dual field automatically

meta_layer.system_note: bilingual EN+JA craft biography and technical guide — photographs by Berton, Perotta, Shino — presentation Augusto Panini
meta_layer.compatibility: NarrativeScene v4.1 / NarrativeSilence v4.1 / NarrativeSystemGraph v4.1

catalog_connection.assolutamente_giappone: same Japan field — cultural translation through craft and fashion
catalog_connection.la_singolarita_giappone: same Japan as irresolvable singularity — philosophical vs material register
catalog_connection.luna_piena_sulla_citta_antica: bilingual EN+JA vs IT+JA — different cultural mediations of the Japan field
